narrative: A short (2.7 km, ~10 min) intra-town shuttle inside Budgam. Endpoint clustering couldn't form a robust terminal here (only 2 distinct drivers used it), but path-shape clustering found 16 repeat runs riding the identical line, and it is study-area-clipped as inside Kashmir Division. IMPORTANT: Budgam itself is heavily permitted (42 raw permits terminate there, mostly Parimpora/Jehangir Chowk trunk services) — but none of those permits name 'Chowderi Bagh' specifically, and the permit register is O-D pair level, not stop level, so it would not capture a fine-grained intra-town link like this even if informally served today. Read this as evidence of a real short local movement pattern WITHIN an already-served town, not as an under-permitted gap.
evidence_used: raw-permit registry cross-check (614 permits, 0 exact-name match)
data_quality_flags: only 2 distinct drivers — below the Tier-1 corridor confidence bar (Tier-1 required robust multi-driver terminals)
median run 10 min — too short to separate 'genuine shuttle' from 'errand/personal driving' with certainty
recommendation: Not actionable on its own (2 drivers is too thin to size a service). Worth folding into a wider Budgam intra-town stop/circulation study if driver adoption grows there.
analyst: inline review, Opus (Claude), same session as Tier-1 audit
